what_it_does: Captures an idea for a FACE PIPELINE that takes Tim's free-form Claude Design exports, parses and tags them, composes valid company Vi definitions, validates them, reconciles ambiguities via RHM/Tim decision cards, and registers live company assets.
observations: It is a noticeboard item of type idea, state captured, created 2026-06-18.
It proposes a six-stage pipeline: INGEST, TAG, COMPOSE, VALIDATE, RECONCILE, REGISTER.
It reframes the Claude Design non-conformance problem by moving determinism downstream into translation and validation rather than constraining the design tool.
It identifies the expanded decision-card as a universal primitive for reconcile, seed-react, and decision-surface.
Tim directs integration with all streams and wants the idea brought to the lead and the board.
The next step is a one-asset round-trip scoped to plug into the existing claude-design bridge.
It references build-prep/claude-design/, DECISION-SURFACE-BUILD.md, and finds/ch-2mnxl9j0-VSURFACE-RESEARCH.md.
standouts: The explicit separation of design freedom (Claude Design) from architecture safety (downstream pipeline).
The decision-card reframed as the universal reconcile/seed/decision primitive.
Tim's directive to treat this as the unifying inlet for design intent across all streams.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, created/updated, and history.
Free-form Markdown body with all-caps section headers and parenthetical asides.
Records a TIM-DIRECT in-session note and next actions.
References external files and prior research inline rather than as structured links.
concerns: Heavy reliance on named individuals (Tim, RHM) and session-specific directives may make the item brittle if roles change.
Pipeline stages are described aspirationally without concrete interfaces, schemas, or failure modes.
No explicit success criteria or metrics for the one-asset round-trip.
Assumes existing company machinery (address/corpus/resolution/channels) is ready to consume the output, which is not verified in this file.
notes: A captured idea for a Claude Design-to-company asset pipeline that uses downstream determinism and decision-card reconciliation to turn free-form design exports into live, validated company Vi assets.
questions: What is the exact format of the Claude Design HTML/CSS handoff bundle?
Which existing registry types and Vi definition schema will the COMPOSE stage target?
What is the role and authority of the RHM in the RECONCILE step?
How will the one-asset round-trip be tested and accepted?
What does DNA tokens specifically encompass in this context?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to capture and communicate the FACE PIPELINE idea; the file does that, mixing concept, architecture, process, and next steps.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a unified, deterministic pipeline that accepts unconstrained design output from Claude Design and converts it into validated, registered company visual assets, while using a shared decision-card mechanism to resolve ambiguities with Tim.
novelty: The deliberate architectural split between an unconstrained no-code design tool and a deterministic, schema-bound translation pipeline, plus the elevation of a decision-card as a single primitive for reconcile, seeding, and decision-making.
contribution: It contributes a concrete pipeline concept (INGEST→TAG→COMPOSE→VALIDATE→RECONCILE→REGISTER), a reframe that locates determinism downstream of design freedom, and a proposal to reuse the existing address/corpus/channel/decision-card machinery as the integration surface.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard idea item describing the FACE PIPELINE, a six-stage process to translate Tim's free-form Claude Design exports into validated, registered company Vi assets via deterministic downstream composition, validation, and RHM/Tim decision-card reconciliation.
intention_for_embedding: To propose and align a unifying design-to-asset inlet that integrates Claude Design output with the company's existing address, corpus, channel, and decision-card systems.
